summary: The Omniverse runs a subscription 3D virtual-world platform ('Omniverse City', built on Unreal Engine and playable in-browser) with persistent districts, an in-game OCX credit economy, AI citizens (DigOzens), creator tools, and event hosting. It serves gaming enthusiasts, content creators, and digital entrepreneurs aged 18-35 who want a persistent virtual identity and a way to build, host, and earn inside an immersive world. Users enter free via the Explorer tier and self-serve upgrade to paid tiers (OmniFolk $12.99/mo, OmniPreneur $39.99/mo, and higher) as they hit the ceiling on residences, businesses, and OCX allocation.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding — First Steps in the City
campaigns.objective: Get new Explorer signups to their first meaningful in-world moment (attend a live event, earn first OCX, or step into a district) within 48 hours.
campaigns.trigger: Explorer license claimed / account created. Send email 1 within seconds of signup.
campaigns.segment: New Explorer signups; branch on signup intent (player vs. creator/entrepreneur) where captured.
campaigns.rationale: There is no structured onboarding today and the free Explorer tier IS the trial — the entire paid funnel depends on activating new arrivals fast. The platform feels real only once a user attends an event or earns OCX, so the first session is the whole game.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome from a fellow citizen, set expectations, and drive the single highest-value first action: step into the city and join a live event or eventure.
emails.subject: You're in the city — here's your first move
emails.preview: One live eventure tonight, and you're already on the guest list.
keyPoints: Welcome them as a new Explorer ('We Are All Players') and frame the city as a place to live, build, and earn — not just another game
Point to ONE concrete first action: jump into the next live eventure or walk a district so the world feels inhabited within their first session
Tease that participation earns OCX platform credits (described as credits, never as money or an investment)
Set the cadence: a few short notes over the next two weeks on getting the most out of the city, with a real human on reply

campaigns.name: Explorer → Paid Conversion (OmniFolk / OmniPreneur)
campaigns.objective: Convert active free Explorers into paying OmniFolk or OmniPreneur subscribers by showing what they've already built and what's locked one tier up.
campaigns.trigger: Activation milestone (first event attended, first OCX earned, or repeat session) — runs in parallel with day-7/14/30 nudges for users who hit the free-tier ceiling.
campaigns.segment: Activated free Explorers; branch by intent — social/home-base users to OmniFolk, creators/sellers to OmniPreneur.
campaigns.rationale: Explorer→paid is the core revenue play and there is no conversion email today. With no hard trial expiry, the strongest lever is a usage-review nudge that reflects their real activity back to them at the moment they bump the free-tier ceiling (wanting a residence, higher OCX, or to host).

emails.purpose: Reflect their real in-world activity back, show exactly what OmniFolk/OmniPreneur unlocks, and make claiming a residence the obvious next step.
emails.subject: You've earned your place — now claim your address
emails.preview: Your residence, 800 OCX a month, and a permanent home in the city.
keyPoints: Recap what they've already done as an Explorer (events attended, OCX earned, districts explored) so the upgrade feels deserved, not sold
Spell out the OmniFolk ceiling-breakers: 1 free residence, 800 OCX/mo, full inventory — and name the OmniPreneur path (a business + retail tools + 2,500 OCX/mo) for creators
Anchor on $12.99/mo, no contract, cancel anytime in one click — low-risk because it's monthly
Frame the upgrade as going from visitor to resident: 'a home base your community can actually show up to'

campaigns.name: Explorer Nurture — Proof the City Is Real & Here to Stay
campaigns.objective: Move not-yet-ready Explorers toward upgrade by proving the platform has real community and momentum — directly defusing the longevity objection.
campaigns.trigger: Explorer signup with no upgrade after onboarding (or day-7 low activity); ~5 emails over ~50 days.
campaigns.segment: Engaged-but-unconverted Explorers below the upgrade-ready threshold; branch by player vs. creator interest.
campaigns.rationale: The #1 objection is 'is this alpha platform going to stick around?' — users hesitate to pay monthly for a world that might shut down. The nurture's hero send answers that head-on with resident stories and visible momentum, the single biggest unblocker for this brand.

emails.purpose: Show real residents and entrepreneurs building lives and earning OCX inside the city, proving the community is alive and the platform is being built — the proof + longevity-reassurance email.
emails.subject: Real people are already building lives here
emails.preview: Meet the OmniPreneurs running shops and the residents filling the districts.
emails.cta: See who's building in the city
keyPoints: Lead with concrete resident/OmniPreneur stories — someone who opened a shop, hosted a packed eventure, or earned meaningful OCX through participation
Address the longevity objection directly: show the update cadence, growing district roster, and partnerships (e.g. gaming crossovers) as evidence the city is growing, not stalling
Use 'We Are All Players' / pioneering framing — getting in early on something real that's actually delivering where past metaverses didn't
Soft CTA to explore a featured district or creator space; no hard sell yet, just proof that the world is inhabited and worth committing to

campaigns.name: Win-Back — Come See How Far the City Has Grown
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant Explorers and churned subscribers by countering the 'this platform is dying' perception with concrete new-content momentum.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold (e.g. 14-30 days no login/opens) or subscription cancellation; ~4 emails over ~3 weeks, then sunset.
campaigns.segment: Dormant Explorers and churned paid members (separate message for each); branch on what they used most.
campaigns.rationale: Monthly billing means zero switching cost and the biggest churn driver is the sense that momentum has stalled. A pattern-interrupt 'here's everything new since you left' email is the highest-leverage win-back for an alpha platform whose survival narrative is its retention story.

emails.purpose: Pattern-interrupt with everything that's launched since they went quiet — new districts, events, features, and partnerships — proving the city is more alive than when they left.
emails.subject: The city grew while you were gone
emails.preview: New districts, new eventures, and OCX waiting on you.
emails.cta: See what's new in the city
keyPoints: Lead with the strongest momentum signals since they lapsed: new districts, upcoming live eventures, new creator tools or DigOzen features, notable partnerships
Directly counter the 'is this dying?' read — show an active, growing world rather than a quiet one
Reference what they used most (events, their residence, their shop) and what's changed there since
One-click return CTA; for churned subscribers, note their residence/business and OCX standing can be picked back up

campaigns.name: Omniverse Dispatch — Weekly City Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind, build authority, and keep the community warm by proving steady platform momentum without discounting.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled cadence (weekly to bi-weekly); repurposes existing blog content (updates, creator spotlights, partnerships).
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding (Campaign 1) and route recently-dormant users to Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: Blog content exists but isn't distributed by email. For an alpha platform where 'visible progress' is the antidote to churn, a recurring dispatch is the always-on engine that keeps Explorers and paid members feeling the city is alive between their own sessions.

emails.purpose: The flagship weekly edition — one useful idea plus a momentum digest (events, creator spotlight, what shipped) with a single soft upgrade/return CTA.
emails.subject: This week in Omniverse City: 3 eventures + a new district
emails.preview: Plus the OmniPreneur turning a corner shop into a crowd.
keyPoints: Recurring habit section ('This week in the city') listing upcoming live eventures and the newest district or feature drop
One creator/resident spotlight that doubles as social proof and an OCX-earning idea — 80% value, 20% soft offer
A single primary CTA per edition (attend an event, read the spotlight, or upgrade) rather than competing asks
Keep the fellow-citizen voice and pioneering tone; describe OCX strictly as platform credits, never as money or investment
